actions.action: Add 3 recognizable customer logos above the fold, ideally from the target segment ({vertical}, {company_size} company size)
actions.tradeoff: Requires permission from the customers; generic or unrelated logos can REDUCE trust

actions.action: Add a named testimonial (with real name, role, and company) from a {company_size} {vertical} customer that directly addresses the top objection: '{top_objection}'
actions.tradeoff: Requires authentic customer; fake-looking testimonials decrease trust below baseline

actions.action: Add a case study card above the pricing section showing quantified outcome for a comparable customer (e.g. 'Cut onboarding time by 43% for a 50-person SaaS team')
actions.tradeoff: Requires real data; higher effort to produce

trust_gap_no_security_compliance.problem_signal: audience_vector.buying_stage in ['evaluating','ready'] AND missing_signals includes 'security_compliance' AND vertical in ['saas','fintech','healthcare']

actions.action: Add SOC 2 / GDPR / HIPAA (as applicable) badge in the footer and a dedicated /security page accessible in 2 clicks from home
actions.tradeoff: Cannot display badges without actual compliance; fake badges create legal risk

actions.action: Link to your changelog / security page from the footer of the pricing page — enterprise buyers check these reflexively
actions.tradeoff: None if the pages exist; if they don't, don't link

actions.action: Reduce pricing from {current_tier_count} tiers to 2 (Starter + Business). Eliminate the middle tier — data shows users treating it as a decoy
actions.tradeoff: Loses anchoring effect of 3-tier psychology; some users read 3 tiers as 'legitimate enterprise'

actions.action: Add a 'Which tier is right for me?' 3-question quiz above the pricing table, showing a recommended tier dynamically
actions.tradeoff: Development effort; if too clever, reduces trust

actions.action: Sharpen the differentiation between tiers: replace overlapping features with exclusive, named benefits per tier. Use specific limits ('up to 100 users') instead of vague words ('enterprise-ready')
actions.tradeoff: None; this is a copy exercise

pricing_hidden_or_contact_sales.problem_signal: scenario.pricing.visible == false OR pricing.tiers includes 'Contact us' only

actions.action: Publish an anchor number for the top/enterprise tier, even if it says 'Starting at $X / year'. Hidden pricing causes 40-60% of your target segment to bounce without engaging
actions.tradeoff: Loses negotiation leverage in enterprise sales; competitors can see your floor

actions.action: Rewrite hero headline from '{current_headline}' to one that names the exact pain and quantifies the outcome (e.g. 'Cut support ticket time by 60% for teams of 10-50'). Replace abstract transformation language
actions.tradeoff: Narrower headline can reduce reach to adjacent segments

actions.action: Replace hero video/illustration with a product screenshot showing the actual UI solving the headline pain
actions.tradeoff: Loses 'brand' feel; some teams see this as too utilitarian

actions.action: Replace each feature card's abstract benefit ('Powerful analytics') with a concrete, quantified outcome ('See which of your 40 landing pages converts 3x above average in 8 seconds')
actions.tradeoff: None if claims are real; risky if claims don't hold up

actions.action: Add a screenshot or animated GIF next to each feature showing the actual UI in context
actions.tradeoff: Outdated UI in screenshots undermines trust; must maintain

actions.action: Change primary CTA from '{current_cta}' to an action-specific verb + outcome (e.g. 'Start a free 14-day trial' or 'See pricing'). Remove 'Get started' — it's the lowest-converting CTA phrase in SaaS
actions.tradeoff: Narrows funnel — users who wanted to 'explore' may leave

actions.action: Add a sticky header CTA that remains visible on scroll. Target users who decide after seeing evidence below the fold
actions.tradeoff: Can feel pushy; test on mobile carefully

actions.action: Remove credit card requirement from free trial. Enterprise-gated products keep card, product-led do not. If your target is SMB/PLG, this is a ~30% conversion tax
actions.tradeoff: Higher churn during trial; requires upgrade prompts

actions.action: Shorten first-run setup from {current_steps} steps to max 3. Defer integrations and team invites until after first aha-moment
actions.tradeoff: Users arrive at less-configured state; may increase support load

actions.action: Add a focused FAQ section addressing the top 3 unresolved objections: {top_objections}. Each FAQ answer should be quantified and specific
actions.tradeoff: Long FAQ sections can dilute focus; keep to 5-7 max

actions.action: Add an in-line objection-handler after the pricing section: 'Worried about {top_objection}? Here's how we handle that.' with a 2-sentence credible answer
actions.tradeoff: None; inline objection-handling is higher-converting than FAQ

actions.action: The landing page headline and hero image must exactly mirror the ad promise. If ad says 'Cut invoice time by 80%', landing page hero must say the same — not 'Transform your finance stack'
actions.tradeoff: Multiple ad variants require multiple landing pages

actions.action: Your traffic appears to include a segment that's priced out. Either tighten ad targeting to filter this segment, OR add a graceful low-tier option that captures them as leads
actions.tradeoff: Entry tier may cannibalize paid conversions; test carefully

actions.action: Add a dedicated 'Migrating from {competitor}?' section showing: data import, team migration steps, and a 1-click import tool (or concierge migration service)
actions.tradeoff: Significant engineering investment; worth it for product-led growth

actions.action: Add a direct comparison table: {competitor} vs. {your_product}, with specific feature-level comparisons, not vague 'more modern UX'
actions.tradeoff: Legal sensitivity around direct competitor claims; keep factual
